evidence.scientific_clarity: Ridgeline KDE plot clearly shows 6-cluster expression distributions with visible ordering from low (C1) to high (C6) median expression, fully matching the brief's requirement.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams; pdffonts reveals Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — non-editable, non-vector text; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default and should not be used.
evidence.palette: Uses Tableau-inspired hex palette (#4e79a7, #f28e2b, etc.) which is colorblind-safe for most deficiencies; no directional coloring concern since all ridges are nominal clusters — acceptable but not explicitly verified against all colorblind modes.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,5) with 6 stacked ridges and mild overlap=0.55 fills the canvas well; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels; y-range set to n*row_height+0.2 is appropriate.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: No legend (not needed for ridge labels), y-tick labels identify clusters on the left axis as required; caption matches the figure; minor issue: no explicit color legend/key linking cluster to color, and title could be more informative.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF fonts are Type 3 (bitmap-embedded), not Type 1/TrueType with fonttype=42; text is not selectable; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: All four required outputs exist: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg (bonus), caption.tex, gen_figure.py — all non-trivial in file size.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains KDE methodology (Gaussian KDE, Scott's rule), stacking approach, and provides specific numerical claims (Cluster 1 median ~1.9, Cluster 6 median ~3.3, progressive increase described).

overall_strengths: Clear, readable ridgeline visualization that directly communicates the 6-cluster expression distribution hierarchy with well-chosen overlap and a clean spine-less layout.
Caption is substantive and includes quantitative findings (median values for extreme clusters), methodology details, and a narrative of the trend across clusters.
overall_weaknesses: PDF fonttype is not set to 42 — fonts are embedded as Type 3 bitmaps (DejaVu Sans default), making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors; this is a significant typography and vector fidelity failure.
No explicit colorblind simulation was performed and the default DejaVu Sans font family is used throughout rather than a publication-quality font (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif like Times).
